name: Creation of Alternate Data Stream
description: Detects the creation of an Alternate Data Stream (ADS). ADS is a feature of the NTFS file system that allows forking data streams. Attackers can hide malware, tools, and exfiltrate data using ADSs.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Windows Security Event ID (either 4688 for Process Creation or 4663 for File System Access).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process involved in creating the ADS.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the process involved (if event_id is 4688).

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line if a process created the ADS (if event_id is 4688).

parameters.name: object_name
parameters.description: The name of the object (file) where the ADS was created (if event_id is 4663).

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account associated with the event.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
